decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful control element, with a wide range of multicolored spells to support its strategy. The deck includes a variety of ramp spells, removal options, and creature threats to maintain board control and outvalue opponents. The inclusion of cards like Bring to Light, Leyline Binding, and Vanishing Verse allows for versatile answers to opposing threats, while powerful finishers like Elesh Norn, Omnath, and Niv-Mizzet, Supreme provide the deck with multiple win conditions. The companion Yorion, Sky Nomad adds additional value through flickering cards for extra value.

This Dimir Control deck aims to maintain board control through a combination of removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize, as well as disruptive creatures like Narset, Parter of Veils. The deck also utilizes bounce spells like Boomerang Basics and Cryogen Relic to keep opponents off balance. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value by blinking permanents for extra triggers. The deck's mana base is centered around lands that provide black and blue mana sources, ensuring consistency in casting spells and activating abilities.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, using cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate powerful creatures and overwhelm the opponent. It also includes disruption, protection, and card advantage elements to ensure the combo can be executed efficiently. The deck aims to control the board while setting up its win condition, ultimately generating a powerful board state that is difficult for the opponent to deal with.

The primary focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures in the Selesnya colors. By utilizing cards like Collected Company to put multiple threats onto the battlefield at once, along with disruptive creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain a strong board presence. With a mix of mana acceleration, removal, and powerful finishers like Elspeth, Storm Slayer, the deck is designed to quickly establish dominance and close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on using the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate value and create a strong board presence. It also includes a mix of removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board. The deck aims to create a fast and aggressive game plan with creatures like Fleeting Spirit and Iron-Shield Elf, while also having utility lands like Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire and Geier Reach Sanitarium to provide additional options. Overall, the deck looks to overwhelm the opponent with its synergy and powerful threats.

This deck focuses on utilizing the combo between Greasefang and Parhelion II to create an overwhelming advantage. The deck includes various support cards like Fatal Push, Duress, and Thoughtseize to control the game and protect the combo pieces. Monument to Endurance helps protect the creatures while Fleeting Spirit and Iron-Shield Elf provide additional threats on the battlefield. The mana base includes a mix of lands to ensure consistency in casting spells. Overall, the strategy revolves around setting up the combo and outmaneuvering the opponent to secure victory.

This deck focuses on using a combination of low-cost spells and efficient creatures to apply pressure on the opponent quickly. By playing creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter alongside spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to deal damage rapidly and overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of card draw options like Sleight of Hand and Academic Dispute helps maintain the deck's momentum and ensure a consistent stream of threats. With a focus on tempo and aggression, this deck is designed to close out games swiftly.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly play and pump up low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while also using burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to control the board and finish off opponents. The deck also includes card draw spells like Sleight of Hand and Consider to help maintain hand advantage. The goal is to maintain a fast-paced aggressive strategy to overwhelm opponents and deal damage efficiently, ultimately securing victory through a swift and decisive playstyle.

The main focus of this deck is to maintain a fast-paced and aggressive playstyle, utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck includes a mix of burn spells, creatures with prowess abilities, and cards that can help draw additional resources. By applying early pressure and controlling the board with tempo plays, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish a strong defense. The inclusion of cards like Academic Dispute and Flow State adds versatility and disruption to keep the opponent off balance.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to aggressively play creatures and spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck includes cards that can bounce enemy creatures, counter spells, draw cards, and buff your own creatures. It focuses on tempo and generating card advantage while maintaining pressure on the opponent. Key cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Combustion Technique help you maintain board presence and deal damage efficiently. The deck aims to control the battlefield and overwhelm the opponent with fast-paced aggression.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay. It also aims to control the game by utilizing powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance, as well as utilizing graveyard recursion with cards like Graveyard Trespasser. The deck is designed to outvalue the opponent in the long game, while maintaining a strong board presence with creatures like Badgermole Cub and Mutavault.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive creature-based strategy. By utilizing efficient mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, the deck aims to quickly ramp into threats like Archon of Emeria, Skyclave Apparition, and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. Collected Company provides additional creature advantage while cards like Enduring Innocence and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes versatile spells like Aven Interrupter and Ouroboroid to maintain pressure and control the battlefield.

The deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II as key combo pieces to create a powerful synergy. It includes support cards like Monument to Endurance and Fleeting Spirit to enhance the combo's effectiveness. Additionally, it includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game and protect the combo. The deck also incorporates a mix of lands to ensure consistent mana production and features specific cards like Iron-Shield Elf and Vanishing Verse to deal with threats efficiently. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm opponents with its synergistic gameplay.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to maintain a fast-paced aggressive strategy, utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Cori-Steel Cutter to deal damage quickly. The deck also includes spells like Reckless Rage and Burst Lightning to remove threats from the opponent's board while continuing to apply pressure. With a focus on tempo and aggression, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game and secure a swift victory. Additionally, the inclusion of disruptive spells like Spell Pierce and Prismari Charm adds versatility to the deck's game plan.

The main idea behind this deck is to control the game by disrupting your opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, while also efficiently ramping up your own resources with mana dorks like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The deck also utilizes powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance to pressure your opponent and finish the game. Additionally, it includes removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Witherbloom Charm to deal with problematic permanents. The key is to outvalue your opponent and grind out a win through efficient control and powerful threats.

The main focus of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. The deck includes powerful creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria to disrupt the opponent's game plan. With cards like Collected Company and Elspeth, Storm Slayer, the deck can quickly generate a board presence and apply pressure. The mana base is optimized for consistency and allows for quick deployment of threats. Overall, the deck aims to win the game quickly by putting the opponent on the back foot and maintaining a strong board presence.
